/cwe/weakness/202.Name: Exposure of Sensitive Information Through Data Queries
/cwe/weakness/202.Description: When trying to keep information confidential, an attacker can often infer some of the information by using statistics.
/cwe/weakness/202.ExtendedDescription: In situations where data should not be tied to individual users, but a large number of users should be able to make queries that "scrub" the identity of users, it may be possible to get information about a user -- e.g., by specifying search terms that are known to be unique to that user.

CommonConsequences.Note: Sensitive information may possibly be leaked through data queries accidentally.

Entries.IntroText: See the book Translucent Databases for examples.

PotentialMitigations.Description: This is a complex topic. See the book Translucent Databases for a good discussion of best practices.
